name: Band Reverse Wrist Curl
resistance_profile: Elastic band tension rises as the wrist extends, so the wrist extensors work hardest in the fully shortened, contracted position.
cues: Only the wrist moves, the forearm stays down
Drive the knuckles up as high as possible
Resist the band on the way down
execution_steps: Anchor one end of the band under your foot and take the other end with a palm-down, pronated grip.
Rest the forearm on your thigh or a bench with the wrist past the edge, knuckles pointing down.
Extend the wrist upward against the band, lifting the knuckles toward the ceiling.
Lower slowly back to full flexion while keeping tension on the band.
good_when: You want to train the often-neglected wrist extensors for elbow and grip health
You are training at home or traveling with only bands
bad_when: You want a stretch-biased extensor exercise, since band tension is lightest at the stretch
You need heavy load the band cannot supply
common_errors: Letting the band snap the wrist back down
Moving the whole forearm instead of just the wrist
progressions: Use a thicker band or shorten the band for more tension
Add a peak-contraction hold at the top
